properties.remark: REPORTS OF A FARM SHED DOWN AND POWERLINES DOWN.
REPORTS OF MULTIPLE TREES DOWN.
EF1 TORNADO. SHORT TRACK TORNADO OF ONE HALF MILE WITH MAX WIND SPEED OF 95 MPH. DAMAGE TO TREES AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS.
SHORT TRACK TORNADO OF ONE HALF MILE WITH MAX WIND SPEED OF 95 MPH. DAMAGE TO TREES AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS.

EF1 TORNADO. PATH LENGTH OF 12.8 MILES AND MAX WIND SPEED OF 100 MPH. PATH EXTENDS FROM WEST OF BELMONT TO JUST SOUTHWEST OF MINERAL POINT. NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. DAMAGE TO SEVERAL FARM BUILDINGS, OUTBUILDINGS AND GRAIN BINS.

PATH LENGTH OF 12.8 MILES AND MAX WIND SPEED OF 100 MPH. PATH EXTENDS FROM WEST OF BELMONT TO JUST SOUTHWEST OF MINERAL POINT. NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. DAMAGE TO SEVERAL FARM BUILDINGS, OUTBUILDINGS AND GRAIN BINS,.

HAVE SEPARATED THE PATH OF THE BELMONT TO MINERAL POINT TORNADO INTO TWO SEPARATE PATHS BASED UPON RADAR DATA AND THE LACK OF DAMAGE BETWEEN THE SOUTHERN TRACK AND NORTHERN TRACK. EF-1 RATING FOR THE SECOND TRACK WHICH STARTED JUST NORTHWEST OF HIGHWAY 151 AND PARALLELED IT TOWARD MINERAL POINT. PATH LENGTH: 6.65 MI. WIDTH: 75YD. EST PEAK WIND 95 MPH. MAIN DAMAGE WAS TO A NUMBER OF FARM STRUCTURES.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE REPORTED NEAR INTERSECTION OF TOWN HALL ROAD AND KOCH ROAD. TIME EST BY RADAR.

EF0 TORNADO. PATH LENGTH OF 2.9 MILES AND MAX WIND OF 80 MPH. PATH EXTENDED FROM 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF JUDA TO 2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF JUDA. LOTS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TOPPED, WITH SOME TREES UPROOTED. MANY LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN. THERE WAS MINOR FASCIA DAMAGE ON A HOUSE.

EF1 TORNADO. PATH LENGTH OF 9.8 MILES AND MAX WIND OF 100 MPH. PATH EXTENDED FROM 4 MILES SOUTH OF JUDA TO 2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF ALBANY. FOUR POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED. MANY OUTBUILDINGS HAD ROOF DAMAGE. ROOFING MATERIAL BLOWN 100 TO 300 YARDS DOWNSTREAM. HAY CART ROLLED ABOUT 50 YARDS. LOTS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TOPPED, WITH SOME TREES UPROOTED. MANY LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN.
ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL STRUCTURES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD H AND WILLOW BEND ROAD SOUTH OF ELKHORN. TIME EST FROM RADAR.

EF0 TORNADO. PATH LENGTH OF 5.7 MILES AND MAX WIND OF 80 MPH. PATH EXTENDED FROM 4 MILES NORTHWEST OF BRODHEAD TO 7 MILES SOUTHWEST OF EVANSVILLE. LOTS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TOPPED, WITH SOME TREES UPROOTED. MANY LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN. THERE WAS MINOR FASCIA DAMAGE ON A HOUSE.
REPORTS OF MULTIPLE TREES DOWN AND POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS EASTERN AND SOUTHEAST GREEN CO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
NWS STORM SURVEY SHOWED DOWNBURST WINDS, WITH DAMAGE IN A WNW TO N DIRECTION.
DELAYED REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF A COLLAPSED GARAGE.

EF-0 TORNADO. ESTIMATED MAX WIND SPEED: 80MPH. PATH LENGTH: 3.8 MILES. PATH WIDTH: 100 YARDS. TORNADO STARTED IN THE CENTER OF BELOIT IN THE MERRILL PARK AREA. THE TORNADO UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES ALONG ITS PATH THROUGH THE EASTERN SIDE OF THE CITY (TURTLE AREA). THE PATH THEN WENT EAST AND APPEARS TO HAVE CROSSED I-39 BEFORE ENDING IN A FIELD.

EF1 TORNADO. PATH LENGTH 4.2 MILES AND MAX WIND 100 MPH. PATH EXTENDED FROM 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF STOUGHTON TO 6 MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF STOUGHTON. TWO POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED. SHEET METAL WAS CARRIED 400 YARDS DOWNSTREAM. LOTS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TOPPED, WITH SOME TREES UPROOTED. MANY LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN.
DELAYED REPORT. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR. A COUPLE OF DOWNED TREES AND A FENCE GATE, WHICH WAS CLOSED AND LATCHED, WAS TAKEN OFF ITS HINGES AND THROWN ABOUT 25 FEET INTO THE NEARBY YARD.

EF0 TORNADO. PATH LENGTH OF 4.8 MILES AND MAX WIND SPEED OF 80 MPH. PATH EXTENDS FROM 10 MILES SOUTH OF WHITEWATER TO 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF WHITEWATER. LOTS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TOPPED, WITH SOME TREES UPROOTED. MANY LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN. THERE WAS MINOR FASCIA DAMAGE ON A HOUSE.

EF0 TORNADO. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEEDS: 80MPH. PATH LENGTH: 10.43MI. PATH WIDTH: 150YDS. PATH FOR THIS TORNADO MAY HAVE STARTED IN ILLINOIS. WENT THROUGH RURAL AREAS WITH THE MAIN DAMAGE TO ROOFING MATERIALS ON A FEW BARNS. TORNADO MAY HAVE BRIEFLY WEAKENED BEFORE GOING THROUGH THE SOUTHERN END OF DELAVAN LAKE WHERE IT KNOCKED DOWN A NUMBER OF LARGE TREES.
REPORTS OF DAMAGE TO MULTIPLE BUILDINGS BETWEEN SHARON AND DELEVAN.

EF0 TORNADO. PATH LENGTH 1.4 MILES AND MAX WIND 80 MPH. PATH EXTENDED FROM 1 MILE SOUTHWEST OF LAKE RIPLEY TO THE NORTH SIDE OF LAKE RIPLEY, ALONG HIGHWAY 18. LOTS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TOPPED, WITH SOME TREES UPROOTED. MANY LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN. SOME FENCE DAMAGE WAS NOTED. A SHED WAS DESTROYED. SOME SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF A ROOF. SOME FASCIA DAMAGE TO A HOUSE WAS NOTED.
CAMBRIDGE FIRE DEPT REPORTED SEEING A TORNADO WITH TREE DAMAGE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF US HWY 12 AND SLEEPY HOLLOW ROAD. TIME EST FROM RADAR.

EF-0 TORNADO. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEEDS: 80MPH. PATH LENGTH: 6.1 MILES. PATH WIDTH: 150 YARDS. TORNADO DAMAGE STARTED AT THE GENEVA NATIONAL GOLF CLUB AND WENT DUE NORTH ALONG PRAIRIE RD AND COUNTY RD H. THE MOST SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE WAS TO A STORAGE FACILITY WHICH HAD A HIGH VOLUME OF ROOFING MATERIAL LOFTED ABOUT A MILE NORTH OF THE FACILITY. CONSIDERED GOING EF-1 FOR THIS TORNADO DUE TO THE PERCENTAGE OF ROOFING MATERIAL LOST ON THE BUILDING, BUT THERE WERE NO GARAGE DOORS PUSHED IN AND ONLY A COUPLE OF TREES UPROOTED NEAR THIS LOCATION.
REPORTS OF TORNADO ON THE GROUND NEAR ELKHORN.

EF1 TORNADO. VERY SHORT PATH OF 0.2 MILES JUST SOUTHEAST OF PALMYRA ALONG LITTLE PRAIRIE ROAD. TRACK WAS FROM SOUTHEAST TO NORTHWEST, NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOUSE UNDER CONSTRUCTION.
VERY SHORT PATH OF 0.2 MILES JUST SOUTHEAST OF PALMYRA ALONG LITTLE PRAIRIE ROAD. TRACK WAS FROM SOUTHEAST TO NORTHWEST, NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOUSE UNDER CONSTRUCTION.
RELAYED VIA SHERIFFS OFFICE. TIME EST BY RADAR.
MULTIPLE REPORTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF HAIL AROUND A QUARTER OF AN INCH AROUND THE FORT ATKINSON AREA.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT OF QUARTER SIZED HAIL NEAR ROCK SPRINGS. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS UP TO 80MPH CAUSED A PATH OF TREE DAMAGE FROM THE WEST SIDE OF BELOIT THROUGH THE EAST SIDE OF BELOIT. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED IN A STARBURST PATTERN ALONG THE PATH. OTHER TREE LIMBS AND DEBRIS APPEARED TO HAVE BEEN PUSHED/TUMBLED RATHER THAN LOFTED. MAIN STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS TO A SET OF BLEACHERS AT BELOIT MEMORIAL HIGH SCHOOL AND SOUTHEAST OF THERE AT FAIRBANKS MORSE WHERE A WALL WAS PUSHED IN.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS UP TO 60 MPH CAUSED DAMAGE ON THE NORTH SIDE OF MILTON. A FEW SHINGLES OFF OF A ROOF, LIMBS DOWNED, A KAYAK BLOWN SEVERAL RESIDENCES DOWN, PATIO TABLES FLIPPED, CHAIRS TIPPED OVER, DECK RAILING DAMAGED AND DAMAGE TO A POOL.

EF-0 TORNADO. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH. PATH LENGTH OF 0.6 MILES. ON THE GROUND FOR 2 MINUTES. ESTIMATED WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. BRIEF TORNADO WENT THROUGH THE COBBLE CREEK NEIGHBORHOOD EAST OF LAKE GENEVA NEAR TOWN OF BLOOMFIELD/LYONS LINE. MAIN DAMAGE WAS TO NUMEROUS TREES AT ONE RESIDENCE. HARDWOODS AND SOFTWOODS UPROOTED IN A CONVERGENT PATH WITH SOME DEBRIS LOFTED FROM THAT RESIDENCE INTO A FIELD NORTHEAST OF RESIDENCE. SOME MINOR ROOFING DAMAGE TO SHINGLING AND SOFFIT AT THAT RESIDENCE AND ANOTHER AT THE END OF THE COURT.
REPORT OF NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME RADAR ESTIMATED.

Corrects previous landspout report from 4 SSE Darien. Trained spotter looking west from Walworth at the intersection of US Highway 14 and WI-67 reported a brief landspout with dust visible at the surface under a funnel. Report was relayed via WFO LOT. Time and location estimated based on radar.

Trained spotter looking west from Walworth at the intersection of US Highway 14 and WI-67 reported a brief landspout with dust visible at the surface under a funnel. Report was relayed via WFO LOT. Time and location estimated based on radar.
Delayed report. Time estimated from radar. Calf hutches and large cattle water tank blown a fair distance, door torn off shed, chute displaced from silo, some tree damage.
Large tree down in the area of E. Rockdale Rd in the town of Christiana.
Power lines reported down in Town of Rockdale.
Quarter size hail now falling within this storm.
Tree limbs down in Fitchburg. Via Dane County Dispatch.
Trees down on the east side of Fitchburg.
Power lines knocked down east of Stoughton. Via Dane County 911.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 ENE Milwaukee. Multiple reports of downed large limbs, uprooted and snapped trees. Time estimated with radar.
One large tree snapped an another uprooted. Time estimated with radar.
Whole tree about 15-20 inches in diameter down across the road.
Delayed report. Damage to sheds, a porch which was under construction and trees near Willow Valley Rd.
Large tree snapped causing power outage. Time estimated with radar.
Large limbs down. Time estimated with radar.

EF-0 near Emerald Grove/Avalon in Rock County near CeCelias Golf Course. Video and photographic evidence of rotation making it down from a funnel cloud extending down from the thunderstorm. The rotation went through a tree line and caused some very minor tree damage. Estimated 60mph winds, 25 yard width.
2 trees down. Time estimated from radar.
Tree down on House. Another tree down just north of this location. Another tree down just north of Baraboo. Timing based on radar estimates.
Large tree down. Pontoon boat overturned at dock on Lake Wisconsin. Time estimated from radar.
Several trees down in Okee. Time estimated from radar.

Downburst winds (estimated 50-60 mph) resulted in numerous trees downed in the vicinity of Highways 113 & 78 specifically on Eagle Point Drive, Lakeview Avenue, Lucille Lane and Grandview and West Grandview. Per Emergency Manager, people in Merrimac who have experienced damage can call 211.
Time estimated from radar.

Delayed report from west of Ottawa Lake. About a dozen of tree tops from silver maples and cottonwoods ranging in diameter up to 16 inches snapped off. Additional reports of a 10 inch diameter apple tree blown down. Damage was blown generally in a south to north direction from a downburst. Winds estimated around 50-60 mph. Time estimated based on radar.
Trees down and power lines down along Pond Road. Time estimated from radar. Estimated 60 mph wind gusts.
Delayed report relayed via tv media. Dozens of trees down, all leaning in the same direction as the result of straight line winds.
Trees down at highway Q near Highway 213.
Iowa County Dispatch relayed report of tree down on Jones Branch Road. Time estimated from radar.
Lafayette Co. Dispatch relayed multiple reports of trees down in the Belmont area, along with a grain bin on County Rd G that had been tipped on power lines by wind. Time estimated from radar.
Lafayette County Dispatch relays report of tree down on County Road G and Bethel Grove Road. Time estimated from radar.
Green County Dispatch reports tree down on power lines southeast of Monroe. Time estimated from radar.
Green County Dispatch reports multiple trees down in Brooklyn and along State Road 104. Time estimated from radar.
Reports of trees down near Evansville. Time estimated from Radar.
2-3 inch diameter branches down in Elkhorn. Time estimated from radar.
Trees and wires down from northern Burlington up toward Waterford. Time estimated from Radar.
Reports of multiple trees and wires down across Racine County.
Report relayed via broadcast media. Multiple tree limbs around 10 inches in diameter down on vehicle. Estimated wind gusts around 60 mph. Time estimated based on radar.
Picture via Twitter.
via MPING.

Report relayed via broadcast media. Large tree limb around 8 inches in diameter fell on house. Estimated wind gust around 60 mph. Time estimated based on radar.
Picture via Facebook.
Report relayed from social media.
Reports from MPING and through Twitter.
Delayed report via social media. Multiple large branches with diameters around 4-6 inches downed near Wisconsin Ave and Elm St. Time estimated based on radar.

Delayed report via social media. Numerous reports of damage around the Baraboo area including multiple downed large tree limbs, power poles, and highway signage. Estimated wind gusts around 60-70 mph Time estimated based on radar.
Several large trees down along the northern portions of Lake Wisconsin. Time estimated from radar.
Lone Rock airport observation 67 kt/77 MPH.
Large tree down adjacent to a house on Lake Wisconsin.
Delayed report via social media. Tree with a diameter around 12 inches snapped near base and several large tree limbs down around the Arlington area. Time estimated based on radar.

Corrects time in previous tstm wnd dmg report from Sauk City. Report relayed via broadcast media. Large tree limb around 8 inches in diameter fell on house. Estimated wind gust around 60 mph. Time estimated based on radar.
Delayed report via social media. Several reports of down trees and large limbs across the Waunakee area. Time estimated based on radar.
ASOS station KMSN Madison-Dane County Airport.
Estimated 8 inch diameter tree down on Hwy 73 a half mile north of Hwy D.
Tree down on Woodland Drive. Time estimated from radar.
AWOS station KEFT Monroe.
Delayed report via social media. Large tree limb with a diameter estimated around 6 inches downed on the south end of Beaver Dam Lake. Time estimated based on radar.
Tree down on house. Time estimated from Radar.
Delayed report via social media. Large tree branch with a diameter estimated around 4-6 inches fell onto a nearby residential structure. Time estimated based on radar.
Delayed report via social media from Wisconet Manager. Uprooted tree and several large tree limbs down around US 12 and Hwy 26 northwest of Fort Atkinson. Time estimated based on radar.
Delayed report via social media. Tree with a diameter around 12 inches down on the north side of the river around Pipersville. Time estimated based on radar.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Fort Atkinson. Report relayed via broadcast media. Multiple tree limbs around 10 inches in diameter down on vehicle. Estimated wind gusts around 60 mph. Time estimated based on radar.
Multiple reports from Dane Co Dispatch of flooding around Madison, especially on the west and northwest side of the city with several cars stuck in the water.
4 in Tree branch snapped.
Delayed report via social media. Several reports of downed trees and large tree limbs around the Palmyra area. Time estimated based on radar.

The tornado began near the SW tip of Upper Spring Lake. Numerous trees were topped, snapped or uprooted along Mill Road. The worst damage was near the intersection of County Road Z and Mill Road where most of the trees on the south side of a residence were snapped or uprooted. The tornado dissipated over an open field one half mile east of County Road Z.
Many 4-6 inch diameter branches down along Grand Ave s of hwy 60. Power pole damaged. Time estimated from radar.
Estimated 60-70 mph wind gust at WFO MKX.
Tree fell and took down power pole. Time estimated from Radar.
Multiple trees down on Hardscrabble Rd.
Trees down. Time estimated from Radar.
Delayed report via social media. Top of a shagbok hickory tree snapped a third of the up. Time estimated based on radar.

Delayed report. Damage relayed by Walworth County EM of a barn/outbuilding that lost a portion of its sheet metal roof. All of the debris appears to be blown downward, indicated straight line/downburst winds to be the most likely cause.

Delayed report via social media. Large tree snapped near the base of trunk with diameter greater than 12 inches and landed on nearby residential structure. Additional reports of downed trees and large tree limbs around the area. Time estimated based on radar.
Split tree and another tree down. Time estimated from Radar.
11in Tree snapped on 69th St.
AWOS station K57C East Troy.
Mesonet station SGNW3 Sheboygan. Peak gust.
Numerous trees down in Elkhart Lake.
3-inch tree limbs and power poles broken.
3 trees and power lines down.
Public report with time from spotter submitted with online form. Tree branches down as well as power outages. No visual verification available.
50mph winds reported with power outages in Ripon.
Delayed report. Large tree branch down on a car. A few other large tree branches down. Time estimated from radar.
Mid lake buoy recorded a 45 MPH/39 kt wind gust.
Drains backed up on Springdale Rd, eventually blocking the road.
Many streets flooded in Kenosha and some cars stranded.

Waterspout reported offshore of Shorewood. Mature Vortex with condensation funnel all the way to the water. Time estimated from Radar.
Waterspout offshore of downtown Milwaukee. Mature vortex with condensation funnel all the way to the water and spray around the base.
Waterspout offshore of Cudahy near Sheridan beach. Mature vortex with condensation funnel to water and spray around the base.
Waterspout offshore of North Bay. Mature Waterspout with condensation funnel all the way to the water and spray around the base.
Waterspout spotted near South Milwaukee near Grant Park Beach. Condensation funnel all the way to the water with spray around the base. Time estimated from radar.
Social Media videos and pictures appear to confirm tornado east of New Glarus and SSW of Belleville.
Size estimated from photo of hail on palm of hand reported via Facebook. Comment reported up to golf ball size hail and dented cars on east side of Madison.
